creator: rea-fbsd@codelabs.ru
text:
Document multiple issues that are still real for databases/mysql41-server, since it stays (almost) at 4.1.22. Vulnerable versions of FreeBSD ports for 5.0, 5.1 and 6.0 are at least 1.5 years old, so I am mentioning them mostly for the completeness. Fix: The following VuXML entries should be evaluated and added: mysql -- remote Denial of Service via malformed password packet mysql-server 4.14.1.24 5.05.0.44 5.15.1.20

MySQL developers report:

A malformed password packet in the connection protocol could cause the server to crash.

 CVE-2007-3780 25017 http://bugs.mysql.com/bug.php?id=28984 15-07-2007 TODAY --- vuln.xml ends here --- I would collapse them all into a single entry, but versions of affected products are different for each entry, so it is not possible without cheating and cheating is bad ;) All these should gone when ports/130023 or its variation will be committed into FreeBSD ports tree.--Om1mqUxTnmycfN4tkD23NFCrCchwNwzVUqOssqTx7LI8Gz4E Content-Type: text/plain; name="vuln.xml" Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="vuln.xml" mysql -- renaming of arbitrary tables by authenticated users mysql-server 4.14.1.23 5.05.0.42 5.15.1.18

MySQL developers report:

The requirement of the DROP privilege for RENAME TABLE was not enforced.

 CVE-2007-2691 24016 http://bugs.mysql.com/bug.php?id=27515 15-05-2007 TODAY How-To-Repeat: See http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2007-2691 http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2007-3780 http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2007-5969 and references therein, especially MySQL bug entries.
